type.prompt: do a code and documentation review pass, resolve any issues discovered. task is only finished when we cannot find anything to fix

# Code & Documentation Review Loop

## Overview

This is a reusable review prompt for Ralph TUI loops. It performs a comprehensive code and documentation review pass and resolves any issues discovered.

## Scope

### Code Review
- Static analysis and linting
- Type correctness
- Logic errors and edge cases
- Security vulnerabilities
- Performance concerns
- Error handling completeness
- Test coverage and quality

### Documentation Review
- README accuracy and completeness
- API documentation (if applicable)
- Inline code comments quality
- CHANGELOG updates (if applicable)
- License and copyright headers
- Any documentation rot (outdated docs)

---

## Quality Gates

The following commands must pass after any fixes:
- `{typecheck_cmd}` - Type checking
- `{lint_cmd}` - Linting
- `{test_cmd}` - Tests passing

Replace placeholders with project-specific commands:
- `pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm test`
- `npm run typecheck && npm run lint && npm run test`
- `cargo check && cargo clippy && cargo test`
- Or project-specific equivalents

---

## User Stories

### US-001: Run Code Quality Analysis
**Description:** As a reviewer, I want to run static analysis and linting to identify code quality issues so they can be fixed proactively.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Run `{lint_cmd}` and capture all warnings/errors
- [ ] Run `{typecheck_cmd}` and capture all type errors
- [ ] Review output for false positives (mark those as acknowledged)
- [ ] Create beads for all genuine issues found
- [ ] Fix all genuine issues discovered

### US-002: Review Code Logic
**Description:** As a reviewer, I want to inspect code for logical errors and edge cases so the codebase remains correct.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Review functions for missing null/undefined checks
- [ ] Check error handling is comprehensive
- [ ] Look for off-by-one errors in loops/arrays
- [ ] Verify async/await error propagation is correct
- [ ] Check for resource leaks (unclosed connections, streams, etc.)
- [ ] Fix any logic issues discovered

### US-003: Verify Test Coverage
**Description:** As a reviewer, I want to ensure critical paths have test coverage so regressions are caught.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Run `{test_cmd}` and verify all pass
- [ ] Check coverage report (if available) for gaps
- [ ] Identify untested critical paths
- [ ] Add or flag tests for untested critical code

### US-004: Review Documentation Accuracy
**Description:** As a reviewer, I want to verify documentation matches actual code behavior so users aren't misled.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Read README and verify all commands work as documented
- [ ] Check API docs match function signatures
- [ ] Verify examples in docs produce expected results
- [ ] Update any outdated or incorrect documentation
- [ ] Ensure all public APIs have doc comments

### US-005: Check Documentation Completeness
**Description:** As a reviewer, I want to ensure documentation is complete so new contributors can understand the project.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Verify CONTRIBUTING.md exists (if project expects contributions)
- [ ] Check for CHANGELOG or similar version history
- [ ] Ensure license/copyright headers present on new files
- [ ] Verify setup instructions are complete
- [ ] Add any missing documentation

### US-006: Security and Performance Review
**Description:** As a reviewer, I want to identify security vulnerabilities and performance issues before they reach production.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Check for hardcoded secrets/credentials in code
- [ ] Verify SQL/command injection vulnerabilities are mitigated
- [ ] Look for inefficient algorithms or N+1 query patterns
- [ ] Check for missing input validation
- [ ] Fix or create tracking issues for any issues found

---

## Functional Requirements

- FR-1: All quality gate commands must pass after fixes
- FR-2: No new lint warnings introduced
- FR-3: No new type errors introduced
- FR-4: All tests must continue passing
- FR-5: Documentation must build without errors (if applicable)

## Non-Goals

- This is a review pass, not a refactoring session
- Do not restructure entire modules unless critical issues found
- Do not rewrite documentation from scratch unless severely outdated
- Do not add major new features

## Review Protocol

### Phase 1: Discovery
1. Run quality gates to establish baseline
2. List all existing lint warnings and type errors
3. Note any failing tests

### Phase 2: Code Review
1. Run through US-001 to US-003
2. Fix issues immediately if trivial
3. Create beads for complex issues that need separate handling

### Phase 3: Documentation Review
1. Run through US-004 to US-005
2. Fix documentation issues immediately
3. Flag documentation requiring significant rewrite

### Phase 4: Resolution
1. For each issue found, attempt fix
2. If fix is complex, create tracking issue instead
3. Verify quality gates still pass after changes
4. Document any acknowledged false positives

### Phase 5: Sign-off
1. Confirm all quality gates pass
2. Summarize issues found and resolved
3. List any open items requiring follow-up

---

## Output Format

After completing the review, provide:

```
## Review Summary

### Quality Gates
- Typecheck: PASS/FAIL
- Lint: PASS/FAIL  
- Tests: PASS/FAIL

### Issues Found
- Critical: [count]
- Medium: [count]
- Low: [count]

### Issues Fixed
- [list of fixes applied]

### Open Items
- [any issues requiring follow-up]
```

---

## Notes

- Use `{project_name}` as the name of the project being reviewed
- Use common sense - if something seems wrong, flag it
- When in doubt, err on favor of thoroughness
- Acknowledge false positives in lint/typecheck output with justification
